educationalNotes.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNotes.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. Spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in downstream dense layers.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: All input images are resized to 224x224x3 before forward propagation.

educationalNotes.detailed: Adds 3 rows/columns of zeros to borders, preparing for the large 7x7 stem convolution.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Maintains spatial representation shapes in outer boundary elements.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Ensures stem output matches Keras shape exactly.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 64 independent filters of size 7x7 operating over 3 input channels. No bias is used.

educationalNotes.summary: Extracts basic visual patterns and downsamples spatial size.
educationalNotes.detailed: Uses large 7x7 filters to capture initial visual contexts while downsampling resolution to 112x112.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Captures major shapes and boundaries while reducing computational density.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Stem convolutions reduce resolution for the dense blocks.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNotes.detailed: Balances data scaling across the stem network.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Avoids covariate shift early in training.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes initial features.

educationalNotes.detailed: Gives non-linear shapes to features.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Permits mapping of complex shapes.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Introduces non-linearity.

educationalNotes.detailed: Pads input borders to maintain visual maps across max pooling.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Protects outer boundary structures.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Pads boundaries.

educationalNotes.detailed: Reduces features to a smaller grid size of 56x56.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Saves computing cost for downstream dense block processing.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: MaxPooling downsamples features.

educationalNotes.detailed: Ensures the concatenated representation is normalized before bottleneck projection.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Keeps activations scaled properly across channels.

educationalNotes.detailed: Pre-activation activation function.

educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Pre-activation layout ensures smoother gradient flow.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 3x3 spatial filter producing growth rate channels.

educationalNotes.detailed: The growth rate (k) is 32. This layer only needs to output 32 channels, representing the new features discovered.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Controls model size while adding new visual representations.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Growth rate (k) defines how many channels each layer contributes.

educationalNotes.detailed: Concatenates the block's input (64 channels) with the new 32 channels produced by the 3x3 conv, outputting 96 channels.
educationalNotes.analogy: Like building a sandwich where each layer adds a new slice, keeping all previous slices intact.
educationalNotes.whyItMatters: Preserves features directly across all subsequent block stages.
educationalNotes.keyTakeaway: Concatenation preserves all visual features for subsequent reuse.
